title: Biochemical Tests for Bacterial Identification
description: Complete reference data for identifying bacteria via gram stain, morphology, and biochemical tests. Organized by gram reaction and cell shape.
source: Medical Bacteriology and Mycology study notes (MTMI 530)

notes.coagulase_positive: S. aureus is the main coagulase-positive species

notes.bile_esculin: Enterococcus and Group D Streptococcus are bile esculin positive
notes.PPR: CAMP-like reaction used to identify Group B
notes.candle_jar: Optochin test requires incubation in candle jar (5% CO2)

rows.morphology: Short rod, coccoid, coccobacilli (Mucoid, Pink/yellow colony)
Short rod or irregular rod ± branching

notes.motility: L. monocytogenes shows umbrella-shaped motility at room temperature

pleomorphic_Haemophilus: BA for X and V factor requirement

notes.oxidase: All Gram-negative cocci here are oxidase-positive
notes.CO2: Neisseria gonorrhoeae and N. meningitidis require CO2 (incubate in candle jar ~5% CO2)

notes.non_pathogenic_Neisseria: Grow on nutrient agar; N. gonorrhoeae and N. meningitidis do not
notes.CO2_requirement: Plates incubated in candle jar (~5% CO2)

notes.XV_test: Use Staph-streak method on Blood agar to test for X and V factor requirements

NFB_tests: King P (Pigment on King B agar)
King F (Fluorescent on King A agar)

notes.Vibrio_NaCl: Vibrio requires NaCl for growth (halophilic); tests done with 1% NaCl added
notes.NFB_oxidase_negative: Acinetobacter and Stenotrophomonas are oxidase-negative NFB

notes.V_cholerae_O1: V. cholerae O1 El Tor biotype is VP positive
notes.NaCl: Vibrio spp. require NaCl; tests with VP, LD, OD, AD performed in 1% NaCl broth
notes.Aeromonas_vs_Vibrio: Aeromonas grows at 0% NaCl; most Vibrio do not

table_enterobacterales_key_organisms.title: Key Differentiation of Common Enterobacterales
table_enterobacterales_key_organisms.description: Selected key reactions. Refer to full biochemical chart for complete identification.

notes.MR_VP: MR (Methyl Red), VP (Voges-Proskauer) — opposite results indicate different fermentation pathways
notes.IMViC: Classic IMViC = Indole, MR, VP, Citrate; E. coli = +, +, -, - ; Klebsiella = -, -, +, +

table_NFB_key_organisms.title: Non-Fermentative Gram-Negative Bacteria (NFB) Key Reactions
table_NFB_key_organisms.description: Selected key reactions for common NFB.

notes.glucose_OF: O = Oxidative only; F = Fermentative; - = No reaction (non-saccharolytic)
notes.King_B_agar: King B agar used for pyocyanin (King P) detection
notes.King_A_agar: King A agar used for fluorescein (King F) detection

tests.interpretation: Slant/Butt: K=alkaline (red/pink), A=acid (yellow); Gas=bubbles/cracks; H2S=black precipitate
+ = mixed acid fermentation (red color); E. coli +, Klebsiella -
+ = butanediol fermentation (pink/red color); Klebsiella +, E. coli -
+ = purple color (alkaline); used to differentiate Salmonella, E. coli, etc.
+ = purple color (alkaline)
+ = purple color (alkaline)
+ = green color after adding FeCl3; Proteus, Morganella, Providencia +
O = oxidative (open tube only), F = fermentative (both tubes), - = no reaction
+ = enhanced hemolysis near Staph aureus streak; Group B Streptococcus +
+ = inhibition of hemolysis near Staph aureus; C. pseudotuberculosis, C. ulcerans, A. haemolyticum +
+ = red/pink after adding PYR reagent; Group A Streptococcus +
Pseudomonas aeruginosa produces pyocyanin (blue-green) on King B agar
Fluorescent pseudomonads produce fluorescein visible under UV light
Tests lysine decarboxylase and H2S production together
Combined test: Motility = spreading growth; Indole = pink ring with Kovacs; Ornithine = color change
+ = clear halo around colony on DNase agar with HCl
S. pneumoniae is sensitive (zone ≥14 mm); other streptococci are resistant; use candle jar
S. saprophyticus is resistant (R, zone <14 mm); most other coagulase-negative Staph are sensitive
Group A Streptococcus (S. pyogenes) is sensitive; others are resistant
+ = black color; Enterococcus and Group D Streptococcus +
+ = turbid growth; only Enterococcus among catalase-negative cocci grows
+ = clot formation; S. aureus is the main coagulase-positive Staphylococcus

antibiotic_resistance_patterns.title: Antibiotic Resistance Patterns (Clinical Microbiology)
antibiotic_resistance_patterns.description: Key antibiotic resistance mechanisms and detection methods for clinically important resistant organisms

resistance_types.mechanism: mecA gene encoding PBP2a (altered penicillin-binding protein with reduced affinity for β-lactams)

resistance_profile.resistant_to: All β-lactams (penicillins, cephalosporins, carbapenems), including oxacillin, cefoxitin, nafcillin
resistance_profile.susceptible: Vancomycin, linezolid, daptomycin, tigecycline, ceftaroline (5th gen cephalosporin), dalbavancin, oritavancin, telavancin

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Major nosocomial pathogen; community-acquired MRSA (CA-MRSA) increasingly common; associated with higher mortality
resistance_types.treatment_options: Vancomycin (1st line), linezolid, daptomycin (not for pneumonia), tigecycline; VISA/VRSA rare

resistance_types.mechanism: van genes (vanA, vanB, vanC, vanD, vanE, vanG) encoding altered peptidoglycan precursors (D-Ala-D-Lac instead of D-Ala-D-Ala)

detection_methods.interpretation: ≥32 µg/mL for vanA/B; vanC intrinsic low-level
Growth with 6 µg/mL vancomycin = screen positive
Differentiate vanA (high-level) vs vanB (variable) vs vanC (E. casseliflavus/gallinarum intrinsic)

resistance_profile.resistant_to: Vancomycin (by definition), all cephalosporins, aminoglycosides (high-level), often ampicillin (E. faecium)
resistance_profile.susceptible: Linezolid, daptomycin, tigecycline; quinupristin-dalfopristin (E. faecium only, not E. faecalis); streptogramins

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Leading cause of nosocomial infections; E. faecium more resistant than E. faecalis; associated with prolonged hospitalization
resistance_types.treatment_options: Linezolid, daptomycin, tigecycline (not for bacteremia alone); combination therapy often needed; ampicillin if susceptible

resistance_types.mechanism: Plasmid-mediated β-lactamases (CTX-M, SHV, TEM types) that hydrolyze extended-spectrum cephalosporins and aztreonam; inhibited by clavulanic acid

detection_methods.interpretation: Cefotaxime/clavulanate or ceftazidime/clavulanate zone ≥5 mm larger than drug alone = ESBL

resistance_profile.resistant_to: All penicillins, all cephalosporins (including 3rd/4th gen: cefotaxime, ceftriaxone, ceftazidime, cefepime), aztreonam
resistance_profile.susceptible: Carbapenems (ertapenem, imipenem, meropenem), amikacin (often), tigecycline, fosfomycin (E. coli only), nitrofurantoin (uncomplicated UTI)
resistance_profile.note: Co-resistance to fluoroquinolones, aminoglycosides, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole common

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Most common cause of community and nosocomial UTIs; associated with treatment failure with cephalosporins; clavulanic acid synergy confirms ESBL
resistance_types.treatment_options: Carbapenems (drug of choice for serious infections); nitrofurantoin/fosfomycin for uncomplicated UTI; amikacin; avoid all cephalosporins even if in vitro 'susceptible'
resistance_types.important_note: ESBL producers should be reported as resistant to ALL penicillins, cephalosporins, and aztreonam regardless of in vitro susceptibility results

resistance_types.mechanism: KPC (Klebsiella pneumoniae carbapenemase), NDM (New Delhi metallo-β-lactamase), VIM, IMP, OXA-48 carbapenemases; or porin loss + AmpC/ESBL

detection_methods.method: Molecular PCR (blaKPC, blaNDM, blaVIM, blaIMP, blaOXA-48)

NDM.inhibited_by: Aztreonam (only β-lactam active; but combine with avibactam for some inhibitors)

OXA-48.inhibited_by: Temocillin resistance, often susceptible to carbapenems at low level

resistance_profile.resistant_to: All β-lactams including carbapenems (ertapenem, imipenem, meropenem, doripenem); often pan-resistant
resistance_profile.susceptible: Polymyxins (colistin, polymyxin B), tigecycline, fosfomycin (some), aminoglycosides (amikacin sometimes), ceftazidime-avibactam (KPC), meropenem-vaborbactam (KPC), imipenem-cilastatin-relebactam

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Critical priority pathogen (WHO); associated with high mortality (>40%); limited treatment options; outbreak potential
resistance_types.treatment_options: Combination therapy often required; polymyxins + tigecycline + carbapenem (if low MIC); ceftazidime-avibactam for KPC; aztreonam + avibactam for MBL; consult infectious disease
resistance_types.infection_control: Strict contact precautions; enhanced environmental cleaning; active surveillance; cohorting patients

resistance_types.mechanism: OXA-type carbapenemases (OXA-23, OXA-24/40, OXA-58, OXA-143), metallo-β-lactamases (NDM, VIM, IMP); porin loss; efflux pumps

resistance_profile.resistant_to: Carbapenems, cephalosporins, most β-lactams; often multi-drug resistant (MDR) or pan-drug resistant (PDR)
resistance_profile.susceptible: Colistin (polymyxin E), polymyxin B, tigecycline (variable), sulbactam (unusual activity against A. baumannii), minocycline; rarely: ampicillin-sulbactam (sulbactam component)

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Critical priority pathogen (WHO); major ICU pathogen; ventilator-associated pneumonia, bloodstream infections, wounds; survives on surfaces for months
resistance_types.treatment_options: Colistin + sulbactam or tigecycline (combination preferred); minocycline if susceptible; high-dose ampicillin-sulbactam; nebulized colistin for VAP; eradication difficult
resistance_types.infection_control: Extremely strict contact precautions; environmental decontamination crucial; hand hygiene; surveillance cultures

resistance_types.mechanism: AmpC overexpression + porin loss (oprD), efflux pumps (MexAB-OprM), occasionally carbapenemases (KPC, VIM, IMP, NDM, GES)

resistance_profile.resistant_to: Carbapenems, many antipseudomonal agents; often MDR
resistance_profile.susceptible: Ceftazidime-avibactam (if not metallo-β-lactamase), ceftolozane-tazobactam (sometimes), polymyxins, aminoglycosides (amikacin sometimes), fosfomycin IV (investigational)

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Critical priority pathogen (WHO); common in ICU, cystic fibrosis; biofilm formation; intrinsically resistant to many agents
resistance_types.treatment_options: Combination therapy; ceftazidime-avibactam; polymyxins + other agents; prolonged infusion β-lactams; inhaled antibiotics for CF

resistance_types.mechanism: VISA: thickened cell wall; VRSA: vanA gene transfer from Enterococcus (D-Ala-D-Lac)

detection_methods.method: Brain heart infusion vancomycin screen agar

resistance_types.clinical_significance: Rare but serious; associated with vancomycin treatment failure; hVISA (heterogeneous) may be missed by routine testing

treatment_algorithms.mrsa_bacteremia: Vancomycin (trough 15-20 µg/mL) ± daptomycin; consider combination; infectious disease consult; source control
treatment_algorithms.vre_infection: Linezolid (bacteremia okay), daptomycin (higher doses for E. faecium), tigecycline (not monotherapy for bacteremia)
treatment_algorithms.esbl_infection: Carbapenem (serious infections); avoid cephalosporins; nitrofurantoin/fosfomycin for UTI
treatment_algorithms.cre_infection: Combination therapy; polymyxin + tigecycline + aminoglycoside/carbapenem; new agents (ceftazidime-avibactam for KPC)

symbol_legend.±: Variable (<80% of strains positive, some strains give positive, some negative)
